name: Yaswanth Korada
headline: AI Engineer @ Kroll | Enterprise GenAI, Agentic AI & Advanced RAG | LLMOps, Multi-Agent Systems | Python, FastAPI, Azure AI, AWS
about: AI Engineer at Kroll, building production-grade Generative AI, Agentic AI, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems for real enterprise use cases — not prototypes. I work at the intersection of GenAI, data engineering, and software engineering, designing and deploying scalable, secure, and reliable AI systems embedded directly into business workflows. My experience spans end-to-end AI development: data ingestion, contextual chunking, hybrid retrieval, multi-agent orchestration, rigorous evaluation, and cloud-native deployment on Azure. I specialize in Agentic RAG systems that power intelligent search, incident resolution, and decision support over large volumes of structured and unstructured data — with a strong emphasis on retrieval quality metrics (MRR, hit rate, groundedness) to reduce hallucinations and ensure production readiness. My work consistently reduces repetitive manual effort by 70–90% by embedding AI-driven automation into core business workflows.

cloud: Azure (AI Studio, AKS, Key Vault, Blob Storage)

bullets: Architected and deployed enterprise Agentic RAG platforms enabling intelligent search, incident resolution, and decision support over large-scale structured and unstructured data.

Designed and implemented end-to-end RAG pipelines (ingestion → chunking → embeddings → hybrid retrieval → re-ranking → inference), significantly improving retrieval relevance and reducing hallucinations.
Built multi-agent systems with LangGraph orchestrating retrieval, reasoning, validation, and recommendation workflows for complex, high-stakes enterprise use cases.
Developed secure, scalable AI backend services using Python and FastAPI, implementing OAuth2/JWT, RBAC, and enterprise SSO for high-compliance environments.
Established AI evaluation and reliability frameworks using RAGAS and LLM-as-a-Judge, leveraging metrics such as relevance, accuracy, MRR, hit rate, and groundedness.
Integrated OCR and Azure Document Intelligence into LLM pipelines, automating document-heavy workflows and reducing manual processing effort.
Containerized and deployed AI services using Docker and Kubernetes (AKS), enabling cloud-native scalability and high availability.
Collaborated cross-functionally with data, platform, and security teams across Azure and Databricks to deliver enterprise-grade AI solutions.
Reduced repetitive manual work by 70–90% by embedding AI-driven automation into core business workflows.
Designed and implemented Python-based microservices and REST APIs enabling secure local-to-cloud data synchronization and backup workflows.
Improved system reliability through structured validation, retry strategies, and resilient error-handling patterns.
Optimized backend API performance to ensure stable behavior under network variability and high-throughput scenarios.
Collaborated with Android Multimedia R&D teams (Audio, Video, Display) supporting Android 14 Beta feature and security updates.
Gained hands-on experience with production-grade backend systems, API performance tuning, and enterprise engineering practices.

projects.name: ResolveAI
projects.tagline: Multi-Agent Enterprise RAG for Incident Intelligence
projects.summary: An enterprise-grade multi-agent RAG platform designed for incident recommendation and knowledge-base search. Orchestrated with LangGraph, it routes queries across specialized agents for retrieval, reasoning, and validation — cutting average incident resolution time significantly.
highlights: Multi-agent orchestration via LangGraph with specialized retrieval, reasoning, and validation agents.
Hybrid retrieval (dense + sparse/BM25) with cross-encoder re-ranking for high-precision incident recommendations.
RBAC and OAuth2/JWT security ensuring data isolation across teams and tenants.
Integrated RAGAS evaluation gates and LLM-as-a-Judge to guarantee retrieval quality before production.
Full observability via LangSmith tracing; Azure Key Vault for secrets management.
Reduced average incident resolution time by several hours per week per team.

projects.name: DocGenius
projects.summary: A production-ready intelligent document assistant supporting multi-format ingestion (PDF, DOCX, images, scanned docs), voice queries, and selective retrieval. Delivers sub-second responses with high groundedness scores.
highlights: Multi-format document ingestion — PDF, DOCX, images, scanned docs via OCR.
Selective retrieval using contextual chunking and hybrid semantic + keyword search.
Voice query support for hands-free interaction.
Sub-second P95 latency on typical document corpora.
Dual vector store support: Pinecone (cloud) and ChromaDB (local).
Deployed on Streamlit Cloud with a live public demo.

projects.name: Data Engineering Pipeline
projects.tagline: Scalable PySpark / Databricks / Delta Lake ETL
projects.summary: An end-to-end data engineering pipeline on Azure Databricks using PySpark and Delta Lake, processing large-scale structured and unstructured data for downstream AI and analytics workloads.
highlights: Built scalable ETL pipelines with PySpark on Azure Databricks ingesting from ADLS Gen2.
Delta Lake architecture with ACID transactions, schema enforcement, and time-travel queries.
~70% reduction in manual data preparation time through automated transformation and validation.
Integrated with downstream RAG and ML pipelines for real-time feature and document serving.
Optimized Spark jobs with partition pruning and broadcast joins for cost efficiency.
